divergence: retirement was never invented — lifeguards are 94, offices have nap wings, and the gold watch is a defibrillator
story_angle: deadpan report of a catastrophe that everyone involved considers a great success
headline: Pentagon Declares Total Victory After Accidentally Bombing Own Bases; 97-Year-Old Admirals Celebrate 'Employment Stabilization Success'
deck: As double-shifts commence on the home front, officials toast to 'career extension opportunities' in the wake of airborne mishaps.
article:
Following an errant strike during routine drills, the Pentagon announced complete success after three naval installations sustained damage from misdirected ordnance tied to pacemaker monitoring by senior officers. Adm. Harlan Peck, 103, proudly stated that the incident 'cleared the decks for the next 40-year watch rotation,' signaling fresh zeal for endless duty among his elderly peers.

Surviving crews now face double shifts rebuilding their own barracks under the fresh 'No Downtime' rule. Bureaucratic arithmetic claims the repairs will both fix the wreckage and lock in steady work. A White House memo framed the episode as spawning 2.3 million fresh 'career extension opportunities,' lifting overtime tallies alongside defibrillator distribution—a singular win for staff barred from retirement.

Residents near strip malls and county fairs have invented 'Overtime Bingo' to mark the occasion, filling squares for every hour past the usual forty. Spokesperson Geraldine Muddle of the Department of Labor observed, 'This episode has drawn the neighborhood closer. Watching the elderly stay productive is heartening, particularly when they lack any alternative.' The outlook for combat stays murky, yet domestic paperwork has rarely appeared more promising—or more geriatric.
author: Harriet Click, Senior Absurdity Correspondent

oped.title: Friendly Fire? More Like Career Fireworks
oped.author: Field Marshal Horatio J. Snuffington, Certified Defibrillator Polisher & Lifetime Nap Wing Curator
oped.body: The Pentagon's so-called mishaps are nothing less than genius-level workforce management. By accidentally leveling our own bases, these 97-year-old admirals have masterfully extended everyone's shifts, proving that employment stabilization requires occasional self-inflicted explosions. Retirement remains the dangerous fantasy of the young and shiftless. Critics moaning about 'collateral damage' forget that those bases' roofs clearly lacked the proper patriotic tint, inviting the bombs like cheap hats at a lifeguard convention. My own modest reform would be mandatory radar-deflecting monocles for every officer under ninety, ensuring future strikes target only the truly idle. As someone repeatedly passed over for the corner nap wing by upstart centenarians, I welcome these airborne opportunities; nothing clarifies priorities like dodging one's own ordnance while the gold-watch defibrillator charges.

classifieds.text: Desperate Pentagon contractor seeks 300 able-bodied 90+ year-olds for double-shift base rebuild after 'total victory' mishap; nap wings and defibrillators provided, no retirement talk allowed.
Slightly cratered naval air station for sale—perfect for extended careers! Includes free unexploded ordnance and 97-year-old admirals toasting stabilization success.
Official apology: Pentagon regrets accidental bombing of own facilities but declares it a career-extension opportunity; all personnel report for mandatory victory double-shifts effective immediately.
97-year-old Admiral seeking fellow 'employment stabilization' celebrants for late-night toast; must love nap wings, defibrillators and pretending home-front disasters are total victories.

panels.caption: Admiral Stroud, 97, taps his oxygen tank: 'We bombed our own hangars. Employment stabilized.'
A 94-year-old colonel salutes from his nap wing cot: 'Double shifts for crater repair start now.'
Stroud grins: 'Promotion party at 0600. Bring your defibrillators.'

joke.setup: After declaring victory over its own runways and barracks, the Pentagon briefed reporters on the new doctrine of self-inflicted attrition.
joke.punchline: The 97-year-old admirals explained that with no retirements possible, the only reliable way to shrink headcount is precision bombing of your own payroll.
joke.text: After declaring victory over its own runways and barracks, the Pentagon briefed reporters on the new doctrine of self-inflicted attrition. The 97-year-old admirals explained that with no retirements possible, the only reliable way to shrink headcount is precision bombing of your own payroll.

sponsor_ads.headline: Admiral DefibWatch 2026
sponsor_ads.body: After declaring total victory on your own bases, keep those 97-year-old admirals clocked in with our gold-standard defibrillator that doubles as an employment contract.

sponsor_ads.headline: Victory Relocation Services
sponsor_ads.body: Accidentally leveled your own headquarters? Our 94-year-old crews rebuild it faster than a nap-wing shift change while celebrating employment stabilization.

sponsor_ads.headline: Total Victory Party Kits
sponsor_ads.body: Toast the Pentagon’s self-inflicted win with mandatory nap pods and defibrillator party favors designed for admirals who refuse to stop working.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Balloons extra; retirement void where prohibited.

sponsor_ads.headline: Eternal Employment Board
sponsor_ads.body: Public notice: 100-year-old lifeguards now patrolling every rebuilt base after today’s accidental triumph—apply today or we’ll assume you’re already on shift.

stories.headline: Reconstruction Firms Add Third Shifts After Pentagon Orders 400 New Runways
stories.article:
Lockheed suppliers logged 1,200 extra concrete orders by noon once three carrier groups limped home with pitted tarmacs. Payroll sheets now carry 87-year-old crane operators on staggered twelve-hour cycles at every East Coast yard.

Executives hailed the stray strikes for 'locking in multi-generational hiring numbers' while dispatching another 600 defibrillator watches to job-site trailers.

stories.headline: NFL Owners Extend Regular Season to Forty-Two Weeks to Retain Veteran Linemen
stories.article:
Team physicians added nap wings beside every sideline bench after two starting centers asked for mid-game breaks. Roster rules now treat advanced age as a protected category rather than a cutoff.

Owners said the extension would also steady ticket income by keeping recognizable names on the books for another decade.

stories.headline: DMV Opens 24-Hour Window for License Renewals as Workforce Stays Indefinitely
stories.deck: Eighty-nine-year-old clerks process forms beside portable defibrillators
stories.article:
County offices installed cot alcoves and microwave stations after staff reported exhaustion from unbroken six-day weeks. Renewal notices now carry instructions for next-of-kin to collect belongings from lockers.

Officials noted a 14 percent drop in wait times once applicants realized every clerk would remain at the counter until further notice.

real_world_echo: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers — mirrored via endless elderly military workforce turning any strike into domestic overwork catastrophe treated as policy win
